title: XOR and AND

content.problemStatement:
Alice has an array AAA of length NNN. She is interested in finding the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1\leq i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N and Ai⊕Aj<Ai&AjA_i \oplus A_j \lt A_i \& A_jAi​⊕Aj​<Ai​&Aj​.

⊕\oplus⊕ represents the Bitwise XOR operator.
&\&& represents the Bitwise AND operator.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain an integer TTT — number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the length of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots , A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N and Ai⊕Aj<Ai&AjA_i \oplus A_j \lt A_i \& A_jAi​⊕Aj​<Ai​&Aj​.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 100000
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of pairs (i, j) (1 ≤ i < j ≤ N) such that Ai XOR Aj < Ai AND Aj.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Numbers with MSB 0: two 1's → 1 pair. Numbers with MSB 1: 2 and 3 → 1 pair. Total 2 pairs satisfy the condition.
Only the numbers 2 (10) and 3 (11) share the same most significant bit (position 1). Hence exactly one valid pair.
All five numbers are 1 (MSB position 0). Number of pairs = C(5,2) = 10.

solution.approach: For two numbers x and y, the most significant set bit (MSB) dominates both XOR and AND. If x and y share the same MSB, then all higher bits are equal (both 1), making x&y have that bit set while x^y has it cleared, which guarantees x^y < x&y. If the MSB differs, the highest differing bit makes x^y larger than x&y. Therefore the condition holds iff the two numbers have the same MSB. The solution groups numbers by their MSB index and for each group of size c adds c·(c‑1)/2 to the answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (each element processed once). Overall O(total N) across all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides input storage – an array of size 31 to count MSB frequencies.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # Example generator for hidden test cases (i from 1 to 20)
    if i == 1:
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(1)
    elif i == 2:
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(2)
    elif i == 3:
        print(1)
        print(2)
        print('1 2')
    elif i == 4:
        print(1)
        print(2)
        print('2 3')
    elif i == 5:
        print(1)
        print(3)
        print('1000000000 999999999 536870912')
    elif i == 6:
        print(1)
        print(4)
        print('1073741823 1073741824 536870911 536870912')
    elif i == 7:
        print(1)
        print(5)
        print(' '.join(['1000000000']*5))
    elif i == 8:
        print(1)
        print(15)
        arr = []
        for _ in range(7):
            arr.extend([1,2])
        arr.append(1)
        print(' '.join(map(str,arr)))
    elif i == 9:
        print(1)
        print(6)
        print(' '.join(['7']*6))
    elif i == 10:
        print(1)
        print(8)
        print(' '.join(['536870912']*8))
    elif i == 11:
        print(1)
        print(5)
        print('1 2 3 4 5')
    elif i == 12:
        print(1)
        print(5)
        print('5 4 3 2 1')
    elif i == 13:
        print(1)
        print(3)
        print('1 1 2')
    elif i == 14:
        print(1)
        print(4)
        print('8 9 10 11')
    elif i == 15:
        print(1)
        print(4)
        print('0 0 1 1')
    elif i == 16:
        print(1)
        print(2)
        print('1000000000 999999999')
    elif i == 17:
        print(3)
        print(3)
        print('1 2 3')
        print(4)
        print('4 5 6 7')
        print(5)
        print('8 9 10 11 12')
    elif i == 18:
        print(2)
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(1)
        print(2)
    elif i == 19:
        print(5)
        for val in range(1,6):
            print(1)
            print(val)
    elif i == 20:
        print(1)
        print(15)
        print('123456789 987654321 555555555 111111111 222222222 333333333 444444444 666666666 777777777 888888888 999999999 1000000000 500000000 250000000 125000000')
